title: podman copy  files to the volume with a container
body:
Close #3059
enabls podman to copy files between the host machine and the volume related to a container.


Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body: This does not fully solve the problem as it was written. It works for named volumes only, and not directories bind-mounted in via `--volume` and `--mount type=bind`, and also not for tmpfs mounts (though I'm not sure if Docker can handle those either - needs checking)

docker cp doesn't work for tmpfs.
I made changes to support named volumes, --mount volume, type=bind.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *QiWang19* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM, would like to get a @mheon head nod.
Hold this until the CVE fix lands
I need to re-review once the CVE is fixed to make sure we don't regress
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3214) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
CVE fix is landed. Rebase and we can re-review
@mheon I think this is ready to go in?
